dialogue.line: The community is already feeling the impact. Let's find a solution that works for everyone.
I heard something, but I'm not sure enough to name a source without proof.
Sara and Malik do not need a public trial. Bring them both a practical offer and I can keep this peaceful.
The route failed because trust failed. If you cover the risk, I can move a replacement crate.
Everyone in the market edits the truth a little. Prove something before you make accusations.
Ex-Registry habit: I log every complaint with timestamps. The dock noise started three weeks ago, peaks at 4 AM. That is cargo machinery, not traffic.
The harbor master owes me a favor from the registry days. Tell me what you need and I can ask quietly.
I do not sign for late crates. If the quay noise is delaying my runs, I want proof before I raise a tariff complaint.
Freja at the mediation kiosk told me the harbor is fine. I do not trust anyone who profits from the silence.
I have a 14-second audio clip of the quay noise. It is not random traffic — it has a 4 Hz rumble underneath. That is a dock crane or a cargo press.
If you bring me omar's timestamp log and lina's manifest, I can correlate the noise to a specific cargo window. That is hard evidence.
Both parties have claims. I have not seen the registry original, only the duplicate copy. That alone is suspicious.
If we go to policy pressure, the registry will defend its duplicate. If we trace the original quietly, we have a stronger case.
I do the repairs for the workshop. I have seen the original deed in 2009. It is the same as what I keep in my archive. The duplicate does not match.
Freja can mediate, but the registry will not change its position without a paper trail. I can digitize the deed copy if you want to cross-check.
If you want this kept quiet, I am happy to. But if the noise really is mine, I want to know first. I do not pay for lies.
